text: Abstract: “Everyone Knows AI”: A Critical Review of Polish Public Opinion on Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence (AI) has been increasingly present in the public debate, and its popularity in Poland is growing. However, as many studies have demonstrated, the Poles often misunderstand the term of AI treating it for example as automation or simply as robotics. This paper provides a systematic review of public opinion research on AI in Poland conducted in the years 2017–2023. The author of the article used 12 studies on this topic to reflect the current public opinion concerning AI in Poland. The main results are presented in five key themes: 1) Conceptualization of AI; 2) Human experience with AI technologies; 3) Social acceptance of intelligent systems; 4) Threats related to the replacement of work by machines; 5) Misunderstanding of AI. The main conclusion of the paper is that the Poles have a rather contradictory approach to AI, when they recognise more and more often the importance of AI in the society, at the same time they feel a high level of uncertainty and fear regarding this technology. The Poles will probably have to cope with many challenges related to the development of AI, which in the future will become increasingly present in everyday life.
